{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_a2494a95c677417d8590490a","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage validate\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"fmt\"\n5\t\t\"strings\"\n6\t\t\"sync\"\n7\t)\n8\t\n9\t// ValidationFormatter provides a simplified, fluent API for formatting validation errors consistently.\n10\t//\n11\t// This builder wraps the ValidationError functionality with an ergonomic interface that allows\n12\t// you to construct validation errors step-by-step using method chaining.\n13\t//\n14\t// # Design Pattern\n15\t//\n16\t// ValidationFormatter uses the builder pattern to provide:\n17\t//   - Fluent, chainable methods for readable code\n18\t//   - Auto-generated suggestions when not manually specified\n19\t//   - Type-safe error construction\n20\t//   - Consistent error format across all validation types\n21\t//\n22\t// # Required Fields\n23\t//\n24\t// Only validationType is required (via NewValidationFormatter). All other fields are optional.\n25\t// If suggestions are not provided via WithSuggestions, they will be auto-generated based on\n26\t// the validation type and expected/actual values.\n27\t//\n28\t// # Common Usage Pattern\n29\t//\n30\t// The typical workflow is:\n31\t//  1. Create formatter with NewValidationFormatter(type)\n32\t//  2. Chain With* methods to set optional fields\n33\t//  3. Call Format() to generate the ValidationError\n34\t//\n35\t// # Example\n36\t//\n37\t//\t// Basic usage with auto-generated suggestions\n38\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n39\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n40\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n41\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n42\t//\t    Format()\n43\t//\n44\t//\t// With custom suggestions\n45\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field\").\n46\t//\t    WithExpected(\"required\").\n47\t//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n48\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"email\").\n49\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Email is required\", \"Check user input\").\n50\t//\t    Format()\n51\t//\n52\t// # Extending for New Validation Types\n53\t//\n54\t// To add support for a new validation type:\n55\t//  1. Use NewValidationFormatter(\"your_type\") with a descriptive type name\n56\t//  2. Set appropriate fields using With* methods\n57\t//  3. Optionally provide custom suggestions via WithSuggestions\n58\t//  4. The formatter will automatically include the type in error output\n59\t//\n60\t// # Fields\n61\t//\n62\t// All fields are private to ensure the builder pattern is used correctly.\n63\t// Access them through the public With* methods and Format().\n64\ttype ValidationFormatter struct {\n65\t\tvalidationType string\n66\t\texpected       interface{}\n67\t\tactual         interface{}\n68\t\tcontext        string\n69\t\tresponseSnippet string\n70\t\tfieldName      string\n71\t\tpatternDetails string\n72\t\trangeInfo      string\n73\t\tvalidationDetails []string\n74\t\tcustomSuggestions []string\n75\t}\n76\t\n77\t// NewValidationFormatter creates a new ValidationFormatter for the given validation type.\n78\t//\n79\t// This is the entry point for the builder pattern. The validationType identifies what\n80\t// kind of validation was performed and is used to:\n81\t//   - Categorize the error in the error message\n82\t//   - Generate appropriate default suggestions\n83\t//   - Provide context for error handling\n84\t//\n85\t// # Parameters\n86\t//\n87\t//   - validationType: The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n88\t//\n89\t//     Common validation types include:\n90\t//     - \"status_code\": HTTP status code validation\n91\t//     - \"error_message\": Error message pattern matching\n92\t//     - \"content_type\": Content-Type header validation\n93\t//     - \"status_code_range\": Status code range validation (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n94\t//     - \"cors_headers\": CORS header validation\n95\t//     - \"response_structure\": Response body structure validation\n96\t//     - Custom types for domain-specific validation\n97\t//\n98\t// # Returns\n99\t//\n100\t// A pointer to a new ValidationFormatter instance ready for method chaining.\n101\t//\n102\t// # Example Usage\n103\t//\n104\t//\t// Basic status code validation\n105\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n106\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n107\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n108\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n109\t//\t    Format()\n110\t//\n111\t//\t// Content-Type validation with context\n112\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n113\t//\t    WithExpected(\"application/json\").\n114\t//\t    WithActual(\"text/html\").\n115\t//\t    WithContext(\"API response\").\n116\t//\t    Format()\n117\t//\n118\t//\t// Custom validation type with all options\n119\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field_check\").\n120\t//\t    WithExpected(\"non_empty\").\n121\t//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n122\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"username\").\n123\t//\t    WithContext(\"User creation\").\n124\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Username cannot be empty\", \"Must be 3-20 characters\").\n125\t//\t    Format()\n126\tfunc NewValidationFormatter(validationType string) *ValidationFormatter {\n127\t\treturn &ValidationFormatter{\n128\t\t\tvalidationType: validationType,\n129\t\t}\n130\t}\n131\t\n132\t// WithExpected sets the expected value for validation.\n133\t//\n134\t// This method specifies what value was expected during the validation check.\n135\t// The expected value is compared against the actual value to provide clear\n136\t// error messages showing what went wrong.\n137\t//\n138\t// # Parameters\n139\t//\n140\t//   - expected: The expected value (can be any type: int, string, []int, etc.)\n141\t//\n142\t//     Common types:\n143\t//     - int: Single expected value (e.g., expected status code 200)\n144\t//     - []int: Multiple acceptable values (e.g., []int{200, 201, 204})\n145\t//     - string: Expected pattern or literal value (e.g., \"application/json\")\n146\t//\n147\t// # Returns\n148\t//\n149\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n150\t//\n151\t// # Example\n152\t//\n153\t//\t// Single expected value\n154\t//\tformatter.WithExpected(200)\n155\t//\n156\t//\t// Multiple acceptable values\n157\t//\tformatter.WithExpected([]int{200, 201, 204})\n158\t//\n159\t//\t// String pattern\n160\t//\tformatter.WithExpected(\"application/json\")\n161\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithExpected(expected interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n162\t\tvf.expected = expected\n163\t\treturn vf\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\t// WithActual sets the actual value received during validation.\n167\t//\n168\t// This method specifies what value was actually received during the validation check.\n169\t// The actual value is compared against the expected value to show the discrepancy\n170\t// that caused the validation failure.\n171\t//\n172\t// # Parameters\n173\t//\n174\t//   - actual: The actual value received (can be any type: int, string, etc.)\n175\t//\n176\t//     Common types:\n177\t//     - int: Actual numeric value (e.g., actual status code 404)\n178\t//     - string: Actual message or content received\n179\t//     - Other types as appropriate for the validation context\n180\t//\n181\t// # Returns\n182\t//\n183\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n184\t//\n185\t// # Example\n186\t//\n187\t//\t// Status code that was received\n188\t//\tformatter.WithActual(404)\n189\t//\n190\t//\t// Error message that was received\n191\t//\tformatter.WithActual(\"access denied\")\n192\t//\n193\t//\t// Content-Type that was received\n194\t//\tformatter.WithActual(\"text/html\")\n195\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithActual(actual interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n196\t\tvf.actual = actual\n197\t\treturn vf\n198\t}\n199\t\n200\t// WithContext sets additional context about the validation operation.\n201\t//\n202\t// Context provides valuable information about where or when the validation occurred,\n203\t// making errors easier to debug and understand. This is especially useful in logs\n204\t// and test output where multiple validations might be happening.\n205\t//\n206\t// # Parameters\n207\t//\n208\t//   - context: A descriptive string explaining the validation context\n209\t//\n210\t//     Common context examples:\n211\t//     - HTTP endpoints: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n212\t//     - Operation descriptions: \"OAuth token validation\", \"User creation\"\n213\t//     - Test scenarios: \"Positive case test\", \"Negative case test\"\n214\t//     - Component names: \"Authentication service\", \"Database query\"\n215\t//\n216\t// # Returns\n217\t//\n218\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n219\t//\n220\t// # Example\n221\t//\n222\t//\t// HTTP endpoint context\n223\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\")\n224\t//\n225\t//\t// Operation context\n226\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"OAuth token validation\")\n227\t//\n228\t//\t// Test scenario context\n229\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"Testing unauthorized access\")\n230\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter {\n231\t\tvf.context = context\n232\t\treturn vf\n233\t}\n234\t\n235\t// WithResponseSnippet sets a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n236\t//\n237\t// Response snippets provide crucial debugging information by showing the actual\n238\t// content that caused the validation failure. This is particularly useful when\n239\t// debugging complex response structures or unexpected error messages.\n240\t//\n241\t// # Parameters\n242\t//\n243\t//   - snippet: A truncated excerpt from the response (recommended: 100-200 characters)\n244\t//\n245\t//     Best practices:\n246\t//     - Keep snippets concise (100-200 characters recommended)\n247\t//     - Include relevant parts that show the problem\n248\t//     - Truncate long responses with \"...\" to keep errors readable\n249\t//     - Sanitize sensitive data before including in snippets\n250\t//\n251\t// # Returns\n252\t//\n253\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n254\t//\n255\t// # Example\n256\t//\n257\t//\t// Short JSON response snippet\n258\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"code\": \"AUTH_001\"}`)\n259\t//\n260\t//\t// Truncated HTML snippet\n261\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`<html><body>Error: Page not found...</body></html>`)\n262\t//\n263\t//\t// Error message snippet\n264\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(\"Access denied: insufficient permissions for resource\")\n265\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter {\n266\t\tvf.responseSnippet = snippet\n267\t\treturn vf\n268\t}\n269\t\n270\t// WithFieldName sets the specific field name where the error was found.\n271\t//\n272\t// This method identifies the exact field that failed validation, which is especially\n273\t// useful when validating structured data like JSON responses, form fields, or\n274\t// configuration objects.\n275\t//\n276\t// # Parameters\n277\t//\n278\t//   - fieldName: The name of the field where the error occurred\n279\t//\n280\t//     Common examples:\n281\t//     - Response body fields: \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"code\"\n282\t//     - Form fields: \"email\", \"password\", \"username\"\n283\t//     - Nested fields: \"user.email\", \"data.items[0].id\"\n284\t//     - Headers: \"Content-Type\", \"Authorization\"\n285\t//\n286\t// # Returns\n287\t//\n288\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n289\t//\n290\t// # Example\n291\t//\n292\t//\t// Simple field name\n293\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"error\")\n294\t//\n295\t//\t// Nested field notation\n296\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"user.email\")\n297\t//\n298\t//\t// Array field notation\n299\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"items[0].id\")\n300\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithFieldName(fieldName string) *ValidationFormatter {\n301\t\tvf.fieldName = fieldName\n302\t\treturn vf\n303\t}\n304\t\n305\t// WithPatternDetails sets information about pattern matching failures.\n306\t//\n307\t// This method provides additional context when validation involves pattern matching,\n308\t// such as regex validation for error messages, format checking, or content verification.\n309\t// It helps explain what pattern was expected and how the actual value didn't match.\n310\t//\n311\t// # Parameters\n312\t//\n313\t//   - details: Information about the pattern that failed to match\n314\t//\n315\t//     Common examples:\n316\t//     - Regex patterns: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n317\t//     - Format requirements: \"expected format 'user@domain.com'\"\n318\t//     - Pattern descriptions: \"must contain at least one uppercase letter\"\n319\t//     - Match details: \"pattern matched at position 5 but failed validation\"\n320\t//\n321\t// # When to Use\n322\t//\n323\t// Use this method when:\n324\t//   - Validating error messages against regex patterns\n325\t//   - Checking string formats (email, phone, UUID, etc.)\n326\t//   - Verifying content patterns in responses\n327\t//   - Any validation involving pattern matching\n328\t//\n329\t// # Returns\n330\t//\n331\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n332\t//\n333\t// # Example\n334\t//\n335\t//\t// Regex pattern explanation\n336\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\")\n337\t//\n338\t//\t// Format requirement\n339\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"expected format: YYYY-MM-DD\")\n340\t//\n341\t//\t// Content pattern description\n342\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"must contain at least 8 characters\")\n343\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithPatternDetails(details string) *ValidationFormatter {\n344\t\tvf.patternDetails = details\n345\t\treturn vf\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// WithRangeInfo sets range boundaries for range validation failures.\n349\t//\n350\t// This method provides context when validating against ranges, such as HTTP status\n351\t// code ranges (4xx, 5xx), numeric ranges, or value intervals. It helps explain the\n352\t// expected boundaries and what range the actual value fell outside of.\n353\t//\n354\t// # Parameters\n355\t//\n356\t//   - info: Description of the range boundaries\n357\t//\n358\t//     Common examples:\n359\t//     - Status code ranges: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n360\t//     - Numeric ranges: \"0-100\", \"1-1000\"\n361\t//     - Descriptive ranges: \"positive numbers only\", \"non-negative integers\"\n362\t//\n363\t// # When to Use\n364\t//\n365\t// Use this method when:\n366\t//   - Validating HTTP status code ranges (e.g., expecting 4xx, got 2xx)\n367\t//   - Checking numeric value ranges (e.g., age 0-120)\n368\t//   - Verifying values within intervals (e.g., percentage 0-100)\n369\t//   - Any validation involving range constraints\n370\t//\n371\t// # Returns\n372\t//\n373\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n374\t//\n375\t// # Example\n376\t//\n377\t//\t// Status code range\n378\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"400-499 (Client Error)\")\n379\t//\n380\t//\t// Numeric range\n381\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"0-120\")\n382\t//\n383\t//\t// Descriptive range\n384\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"positive integers only\")\n385\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithRangeInfo(info string) *ValidationFormatter {\n386\t\tvf.rangeInfo = info\n387\t\treturn vf\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// WithValidationDetails adds additional validation-specific details.\n391\t//\n392\t// This method allows you to provide granular, multi-line information about what was\n393\t// checked during validation and specifically what failed. This is useful for complex\n394\t// validations that check multiple conditions or have detailed requirements.\n395\t//\n396\t// # Parameters\n397\t//\n398\t//   - details: Variable number of detail strings to add to the error\n399\t//\n400\t//     Common uses:\n401\t//     - List specific conditions that failed\n402\t//     - Explain validation rules in detail\n403\t//     - Provide step-by-step validation results\n404\t//     - Document intermediate validation states\n405\t//\n406\t// # When to Use\n407\t//\n408\t// Use this method when:\n409\t//   - Validation involves multiple checks or conditions\n410\t//   - You need to explain complex validation rules\n411\t//   - Detailed debugging information would be helpful\n412\t//   - The validation logic is non-trivial\n413\t//\n414\t// # Returns\n415\t//\n416\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n417\t//\n418\t// # Example\n419\t//\n420\t//\t// Multiple specific failures\n421\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n422\t//\t    \"Password must be at least 8 characters\",\n423\t//\t    \"Password must contain uppercase letters\",\n424\t//\t    \"Password must contain special characters\",\n425\t//\t)\n426\t//\n427\t//\t// Detailed explanation\n428\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n429\t//\t    \"Expected 200-299 status range\",\n430\t//\t    \"Got 500 indicating server error\",\n431\t//\t    \"Server logs show database connection failure\",\n432\t//\t)\n433\t//\n434\t//\t// Step-by-step validation\n435\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n436\t//\t    \"Step 1: Check format - PASSED\",\n437\t//\t    \"Step 2: Check length - FAILED\",\n438\t//\t    \"Step 3: Check content - SKIPPED\",\n439\t//\t)\n440\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n441\t\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\n442\t\treturn vf\n443\t}\n444\t\n445\t// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for fixing the validation failure.\n446\t//\n447\t// This method allows you to provide domain-specific, actionable recommendations for\n448\t// resolving the validation error. Custom suggestions override the auto-generated\n449\t// suggestions that would normally be created based on the validation type and values.\n450\t//\n451\t// # Parameters\n452\t//\n453\t//   - suggestions: Variable number of suggestion strings\n454\t//\n455\t//     Best practices for suggestions:\n456\t//     - Be specific and actionable (not just \"check the value\")\n457\t//     - Provide concrete steps to fix the issue\n458\t//     - Include debugging tips when relevant\n459\t//     - Reference documentation or resources when applicable\n460\t//     - Keep suggestions concise but informative\n461\t//     - Order from most to least likely solution\n462\t//\n463\t// # When to Use Custom Suggestions\n464\t//\n465\t// Use this method when:\n466\t//   - Domain-specific knowledge is needed (not generic suggestions)\n467\t//   - You want to guide users to specific solutions\n468\t//   - Auto-generated suggestions would be too generic\n469\t//   - You need to reference internal documentation or systems\n470\t//   - The fix requires specific domain expertise\n471\t//\n472\t// # Returns\n473\t//\n474\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n475\t//\n476\t// # Example\n477\t//\n478\t//\t// Domain-specific suggestions\n479\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n480\t//\t    \"Check if the OAuth token has expired\",\n481\t//\t    \"Verify the token has 'read:users' scope\",\n482\t//\t    \"Contact admin to refresh permissions\",\n483\t//\t)\n484\t//\n485\t//\t// Specific debugging steps\n486\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n487\t//\t    \"Check service logs at /var/log/api/errors.log\",\n488\t//\t    \"Verify database connection in config.yaml\",\n489\t//\t    \"Test with curl: curl -v http://localhost:8080/health\",\n490\t//\t)\n491\t//\n492\t//\t// Reference documentation\n493\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n494\t//\t    \"See API docs: https://docs.example.com/errors#404\",\n495\t//\t    \"Check troubleshooting guide: /docs/troubleshooting.md\",\n496\t//\t)\n497\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n498\t\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\n499\t\treturn vf\n500\t}\n501\t\n502\t// Format creates the final ValidationError with all configured options.\n503\t//\n504\t// This is the final method in the builder chain that constructs the ValidationError\n505\t// with all the options that have been set. The returned ValidationError implements\n506\t// the error interface and can be returned directly or used in error handling.\n507\t//\n508\t// # Behavior\n509\t//\n510\t// The Format() method:\n511\t//   - Uses custom suggestions if provided via WithSuggestions\n512\t//   - Auto-generates suggestions if no custom ones were set\n513\t//   - Includes all fields that were set via With* methods\n514\t//   - Returns a ValidationError ready for use\n515\t//   - Implements error interface for immediate use\n516\t//\n517\t// # Auto-generated Suggestions\n518\t//\n519\t// If WithSuggestions was not called, suggestions are auto-generated based on:\n520\t//   - validationType (e.g., status_code gets endpoint-checking suggestions)\n521\t//   - expected and actual values (e.g., 404 gets resource-not-found suggestions)\n522\t//   - Common patterns for the validation category\n523\t//\n524\t// # Returns\n525\t//\n526\t// A ValidationError struct populated with all configured fields that implements\n527\t// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or\n528\t// test assertions.\n529\t//\n530\t// # Example\n531\t//\n532\t//\t// Complete usage example\n533\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n534\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n535\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n536\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n537\t//\t    Format()\n538\t//\n539\t//\t// Error() method will produce:\n540\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n541\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n542\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n543\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n544\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n545\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n546\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n547\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n548\t//\n549\t//\t// Can be used directly as an error\n550\t//\treturn err\n551\t//\n552\t//\t// Or formatted for logging\n553\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n554\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) Format() ValidationError {\n555\t\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n556\t\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n557\t\t\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n558\t\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n559\t\t}\n560\t\n561\t\t// Generate a concise message from validation parts\n562\t\tmessage := generateMessageFromParts(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n563\t\n564\t\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\n565\t\treturn ValidationError{\n566\t\t\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\n567\t\t\tMessage:           message,\n568\t\t\tExpected:          vf.expected,\n569\t\t\tActual:            vf.actual,\n570\t\t\tContext:           vf.context,\n571\t\t\tResponseSnippet:   vf.responseSnippet,\n572\t\t\tFieldName:         vf.fieldName,\n573\t\t\tPatternDetails:    vf.patternDetails,\n574\t\t\tRangeInfo:         vf.rangeInfo,\n575\t\t\tValidationDetails: vf.validationDetails,\n576\t\t\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\n577\t\t}\n578\t}\n579\t\n580\t// =============================================================================\n581\t// EXTENDING THE FORMATTER FOR NEW VALIDATION TYPES\n582\t// =============================================================================\n583\t//\n584\t// This section explains how to extend the ValidationFormatter to support new\n585\t// validation types beyond the built-in convenience functions.\n586\t//\n587\t// # Creating Custom Validation Types\n588\t//\n589\t// To add support for a new validation type, you have several options:\n590\t//\n591\t// ## Option 1: Use the Builder Pattern Directly\n592\t//\n593\t// The simplest approach is to use NewValidationFormatter with your custom type:\n594\t//\n595\t//\t// Custom header validation\n596\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n597\t//\t    WithExpected(\"X-Custom-Header: required\").\n598\t//\t    WithActual(\"missing\").\n599\t//\t    WithContext(\"API request validation\").\n600\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"X-Custom-Header\").\n601\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Add X-Custom-Header to requests\", \"See API docs\").\n602\t//\t    Format()\n603\t//\n604\t// ## Option 2: Create a Convenience Function\n605\t//\n606\t// For commonly used validation types, create a convenience function following\n607\t// the pattern of existing functions like FormatStatusCodeError:\n608\t//\n609\t//\t// FormatCustomHeaderError validates custom HTTP headers\n610\t//\tfunc FormatCustomHeaderError(headerName, expected, actual, context string) validate.ValidationError {\n611\t//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n612\t//\t        WithExpected(expected).\n613\t//\t        WithActual(actual).\n614\t//\t        WithFieldName(headerName).\n615\t//\t        WithContext(context).\n616\t//\t        WithSuggestions(\n617\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Add %s header to request\", headerName),\n618\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected value: %s\", expected),\n619\t//\t        ).\n620\t//\t        Format()\n621\t//\t}\n622\t//\n623\t// ## Option 3: Use FormatCustomValidationError\n624\t//\n625\t// For advanced scenarios requiring full control, use FormatCustomValidationError:\n626\t//\n627\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n628\t//\t    \"response_time\",\n629\t//\t    \"< 100ms\",\n630\t//\t    \"250ms\",\n631\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"API performance check\"),\n632\t//\t    validate.WithValidationDetails(\"SLA requires < 100ms response time\"),\n633\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Optimize database queries\", \"Add caching\"),\n634\t//\t)\n635\t//\n636\t// # Best Practices for New Validation Types\n637\t//\n638\t// When creating custom validation types:\n639\t//\n640\t// 1. **Use descriptive type names**: Choose clear, descriptive validation type names\n641\t//    that indicate what was validated (e.g., \"response_time\", \"header_presence\").\n642\t//\n643\t// 2. **Provide specific suggestions**: Custom suggestions should be actionable and\n644\t//    specific to your domain rather than generic.\n645\t//\n646\t// 3. **Include relevant context**: Always provide context about where/when the\n647\t//    validation occurred to aid debugging.\n648\t//\n649\t// 4. **Use appropriate field types**: Match the expected/actual field types to what\n650\t//    you're validating (int for codes, string for patterns, etc.).\n651\t//\n652\t// 5. **Consider validation details**: Use WithValidationDetails for complex\n653\t//    validations with multiple steps or conditions.\n654\t//\n655\t// 6. **Document your validation type**: If creating a convenience function, document\n656\t//    it thoroughly like the existing convenience functions.\n657\t//\n658\t// # Integration with Auto-generated Suggestions\n659\t//\n660\t// The ValidationFormatter automatically generates suggestions based on validation\n661\t// type when you don't provide custom suggestions. To integrate your custom type\n662\t// with this system, you can modify the generateSuggestions function to recognize\n663\t// your new type and provide domain-appropriate suggestions.\n664\t//\n665\t// Current auto-generated suggestion patterns:\n666\t//   - \"status_code\": Endpoint and resource verification suggestions\n667\t//   - \"error_message\": Error analysis and debugging suggestions\n668\t//   - \"content_type\": Header and format verification suggestions\n669\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Range-specific debugging suggestions\n670\t//\n671\t// # Example: Adding a New Validation Type\n672\t//\n673\t// Here's a complete example of adding a \"response_time\" validation type:\n674\t//\n675\t//\t// 1. Create the convenience function\n676\t//\tfunc FormatResponseTimeError(expectedMaxMs, actualMs int, endpoint string) validate.ValidationError {\n677\t//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"response_time\").\n678\t//\t        WithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"< %dms\", expectedMaxMs)).\n679\t//\t        WithActual(fmt.Sprintf(\"%dms\", actualMs)).\n680\t//\t        WithContext(endpoint).\n681\t//\t        WithValidationDetails(\n682\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA requirement: < %dms\", expectedMaxMs),\n683\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Actual response time: %dms\", actualMs),\n684\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA violation: %dms over limit\", actualMs-expectedMaxMs),\n685\t//\t        ).\n686\t//\t        WithSuggestions(\n687\t//\t            \"Check for slow database queries\",\n688\t//\t            \"Verify network latency\",\n689\t//\t            \"Review application performance metrics\",\n690\t//\t            \"Consider implementing caching\",\n691\t//\t        ).\n692\t//\t        Format()\n693\t//\t}\n694\t//\n695\t//\t// 2. Use it in your code\n696\t//\terr := FormatResponseTimeError(100, 250, \"GET /api/users\")\n697\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Performance validation failed: %v\", err)\n698\t//\n699\t// This approach ensures your custom validation types integrate seamlessly with\n700\t// the existing error formatting system while providing domain-specific guidance.\n701\t\n702\t// =============================================================================\n703\t// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON VALIDATION SCENARIOS\n704\t// =============================================================================\n705\t\n706\t// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n707\t//\n708\t// Parameters:\n709\t//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n710\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n711\t//   - context: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"GET /api/users\")\n712\t//\n713\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n714\t//\n715\t// Example usage:\n716\t//\n717\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n718\t//\t// Output:\n719\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n720\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n721\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n722\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n723\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n724\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n725\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n726\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n727\tfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n728\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n729\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n730\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n731\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n732\t\t\tFormat()\n733\t}\n734\t\n735\t// FormatErrorMessageError creates a validation error for error message pattern mismatches.\n736\t//\n737\t// Parameters:\n738\t//   - expectedPattern: The pattern that was expected (e.g., \"invalid.*token\", \"not found\")\n739\t//   - actualMessage: The actual error message received\n740\t//   - fieldName: The field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\")\n741\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n742\t//\n743\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n744\t//\n745\t// Example usage:\n746\t//\n747\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\n748\t//\t// Output:\n749\t//\t// error_message validation failed\n750\t//\t//   Expected: invalid.*token\n751\t//\t//   Actual:   access_denied\n752\t//\t//   Context:  OAuth validation\n753\t//\t//   Field:    error\n754\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n755\t//\t//     - Review the error message for specific details\n756\t//\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\n757\t//\t//     - Verify request parameters match requirements\n758\tfunc FormatErrorMessageError(expectedPattern, actualMessage, fieldName, context string) ValidationError {\n759\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n760\t\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n761\t\t\tWithActual(actualMessage).\n762\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n763\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n764\t\t\tFormat()\n765\t}\n766\t\n767\t// FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n768\t//\n769\t// Parameters:\n770\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n771\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n772\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n773\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name being validated (e.g., \"status_code\", \"response_code\")\n774\t//\n775\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n776\t//\n777\t// Example usage:\n778\t//\n779\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\", \"status_code\")\n780\t//\t// Output:\n781\t//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n782\t//\t//   Field:    status_code\n783\t//\t//   Expected: 4xx (400-499)\n784\t//\t//   Actual:   200\n785\t//\t//   Context:  error response check\n786\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n787\t//\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\n788\t//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n789\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n790\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string, fieldName string) ValidationError {\n791\t\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n792\t\n793\t\t// Build range info string\n794\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n795\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n796\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n797\t\t}\n798\t\n799\t\tdetails := []string{}\n800\t\tif err != nil {\n801\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n802\t\t} else {\n803\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n804\t\t}\n805\t\n806\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n807\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, desc)).\n808\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n809\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n810\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n811\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...)\n812\t\n813\t\t// Only add range info if the pattern is valid\n814\t\tif err == nil {\n815\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n816\t\t}\n817\t\n818\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n819\t}\n820\t\n821\t// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\n822\t//\n823\t// Parameters:\n824\t//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \"application/json\")\n825\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\n826\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n827\t//\n828\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n829\t//\n830\t// Example usage:\n831\t//\n832\t//\terr := validate.FormatContentTypeError(\"application/json\", \"text/html\", \"API response\")\n833\t//\t// Output:\n834\t//\t// content_type validation failed\n835\t//\t//   Expected: application/json\n836\t//\t//   Actual:   text/html\n837\t//   Context:  API response\n838\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n839\t//\t//     - Verify Content-Type header matches request body format\n840\t//\t//     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\n841\t//\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\n842\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError {\n843\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n844\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n845\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n846\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n847\t\t\tFormat()\n848\t}\n849\t\n850\t// FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError creates a validation error for min/max range validation failures.\n851\t//\n852\t// This function is used when validating status codes (or other numeric values) against\n853\t// explicit minimum and maximum boundaries. It provides enhanced error messages showing\n854\t// the exact range that was expected and the actual value that was received.\n855\t//\n856\t// Parameters:\n857\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value (inclusive)\n858\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value (inclusive)\n859\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that was received\n860\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation (e.g., \"status code validation\")\n861\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name being validated (e.g., \"status_code\", \"response_code\")\n862\t//\n863\t// Returns a ValidationError with range information and appropriate suggestions.\n864\t//\n865\t// Example usage:\n866\t//\n867\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(200, 299, 404, \"success response check\", \"status_code\")\n868\t//\t// Output:\n869\t//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n870\t//\t//   Field:    status_code\n871\t//\t//   Expected: 200-299 (Success)\n872\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n873\t//\t//   Range:    200-299 (Success)\n874\t//\t//   Context:  success response check\n875\t//\t//   Validation Details:\n876\t//\t//     - Status code 404 is outside valid range 200-299\n877\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n878\t//\t//     - Check if the endpoint URL is correct\n879\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n880\t//\t//     - Ensure authentication credentials are valid\n881\tfunc FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(minValue, maxValue, actualValue int, context string, fieldName string) ValidationError {\n882\t\t// Get range description\n883\t\tdesc := getRangeDescriptionForValues(minValue, maxValue)\n884\t\n885\t\t// Build range info string\n886\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minValue, maxValue)\n887\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n888\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", minValue, maxValue, desc)\n889\t\t}\n890\t\n891\t\tdetails := []string{\n892\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside valid range %d-%d\", actualValue, minValue, maxValue),\n893\t\t}\n894\t\n895\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n896\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minValue, maxValue)).\n897\t\t\tWithActual(actualValue).\n898\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n899\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n900\t\t\tWithRangeInfo(rangeInfo).\n901\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...).\n902\t\t\tFormat()\n903\t}\n904\t\n905\t// getRangeDescriptionForValues returns a human-readable description for a status code range.\n906\t// This helper provides category descriptions for common status code ranges.\n907\tfunc getRangeDescriptionForValues(minCode, maxCode int) string {\n908\t\tranges := map[string]string{\n909\t\t\t\"200-299\": \"Success\",\n910\t\t\t\"300-399\": \"Redirection\",\n911\t\t\t\"400-499\": \"Client Error\",\n912\t\t\t\"500-599\": \"Server Error\",\n913\t\t\t\"100-199\": \"Informational\",\n914\t\t}\n915\t\n916\t\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n917\t\tif desc, ok := ranges[key]; ok {\n918\t\t\treturn desc\n919\t\t}\n920\t\n921\t\treturn \"\"\n922\t\n923\t}\n924\t// FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n925\t//\n926\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n927\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n928\t// your specific requirements.\n929\t//\n930\t// Parameters:\n931\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n932\t//   - expected: The expected value\n933\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n934\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n935\t//\n936\t// Example usage:\n937\t//\n938\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n939\t//\t    \"custom_field\",\n940\t//\t    \"required_value\",\n941\t//\t    \"actual_value\",\n942\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n943\t//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n944\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n945\t//\t)\n946\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n947\t\tvalidationType string,\n948\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n949\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n950\t) ValidationError {\n951\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n952\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n953\t\t\topt(config)\n954\t\t}\n955\t\n956\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n957\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n958\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n959\t\n960\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n961\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n962\t\t}\n963\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n964\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n965\t\t}\n966\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n967\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n968\t\t}\n969\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n970\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n971\t\t}\n972\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n973\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n974\t\t}\n975\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n976\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n977\t\t}\n978\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n979\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n980\t\t}\n981\t\n982\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n983\t}\n984\t\n985\t// =============================================================================\n986\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n987\t// []string\n997\t\tSuggestions        []string\n998\t\t// Category-aware and severity support\n999\t\tSeverityOverride   ErrorSeverity\n1000\t\tCategoryHint       ErrorCategory\n1001\t\tStatusCode         int\n1002\t\tTimeout            int\n1003\t\tSecurityContext    string\n1004\t}\n1005\t\n1006\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\n1007\ttype FormatOption
