false\n247\t}\n248\t\n249\t// isNonEmptyValue checks if a value is non-empty and meaningful.\n250\t// Returns true for strings with content, numbers (non-zero), and true booleans.\n251\t// Returns false for empty strings, zero values, false booleans, and nil.\n252\tfunc isNonEmptyValue(value interface{}) bool {\n253\t\tif value == nil {\n254\t\t\treturn false\n255\t\t}\n256\t\n257\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n258\t\tcase string:\n259\t\t\treturn v != \"\"\n260\t\tcase int:\n261\t\t\treturn v != 0\n262\t\tcase int8:\n263\t\t\treturn v != 0\n264\t\tcase int16:\n265\t\t\treturn v != 0\n266\t\tcase int32:\n267\t\t\treturn v != 0\n268\t\tcase int64:\n269\t\t\treturn v != 0\n270\t\tcase uint:\n271\t\t\treturn v != 0\n272\t\tcase uint8:\n273\t\t\treturn v != 0\n274\t\tcase uint16:\n275\t\t\treturn v != 0\n276\t\tcase uint32:\n277\t\t\treturn v != 0\n278\t\tcase uint64:\n279\t\t\treturn v != 0\n280\t\tcase float32:\n281\t\t\treturn v != 0.0\n282\t\tcase float64:\n283\t\t\treturn v != 0.0\n284\t\tcase bool:\n285\t\t\treturn v\n286\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n287\t\t\treturn len(v) > 0\n288\t\tcase []interface{}:\n289\t\t\treturn len(v) > 0\n290\t\tdefault:\n291\t\t\treturn true\n292\t\t}\n293\t}\n294\t\n295\t// CORSConfig specifies expected CORS header values for validation.\n296\ttype CORSConfig struct {\n297\t\t// AllowOrigin is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Origin.\n298\t\t// Use \"*\" for wildcard CORS, or specify an exact origin like \"https://example.com\".\n299\t\t// If empty, the origin header is not validated.\n300\t\tAllowOrigin string\n301\t\n302\t\t// AllowMethods is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Methods.\n303\t\t// If empty, the methods header is not validated.\n304\t\tAllowMethods string\n305\t\n306\t\t// AllowHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Headers.\n307\t\t// If empty, the headers header is not validated.\n308\t\tAllowHeaders string\n309\t\n310\t\t// AllowCredentials is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Credentials.\n311\t\t// If true, expects \"true\"; if false, expects \"true\" or header not present.\n312\t\t// This is typically used with specific origins (not wildcard).\n313\t\tAllowCredentials bool\n314\t\n315\t\t// ExposeHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Expose-Headers.\n316\t\t// If empty, the expose headers header is not validated.\n317\t\tExposeHeaders string\n318\t\n319\t\t// MaxAge is the expected value for Access-Control-Max-Age.\n320\t\t// If empty, the max-age header is not validated.\n321\t\tMaxAge string\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t// CORSHeadersIsValid validates CORS headers on an HTTP response, particularly for error responses.\n325\t// It checks the presence and values of common CORS headers and returns whether they match the expected configuration.\n326\t//\n327\t// This function is particularly useful for validating that error responses (4xx/5xx) include proper CORS headers,\n328\t// which is required for browsers to receive and process error responses from cross-origin requests.\n329\t//\n330\t// Parameters:\n331\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n332\t//   - config: Expected CORS configuration (use nil for basic validation that only checks header presence)\n333\t//\n334\t// Returns true if all specified CORS headers are present and match expected values, false otherwise.\n335\t// Returns false if the response is nil.\n336\t//\n337\t// When config is nil, only validates that Access-Control-Allow-Origin header exists (non-empty).\n338\t// When config is provided, validates only the fields that are non-empty in the config.\n339\t//\n340\t// Example usage:\n341\t//\n342\t//\t// Basic validation - check if CORS headers exist\n343\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, nil) {\n344\t//\t    // CORS headers are present\n345\t//\t}\n346\t//\n347\t//\t// Validate specific origin\n348\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\"}\n349\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n350\t//\t    // CORS headers match expected origin\n351\t//\t}\n352\t//\n353\t//\t// Validate wildcard CORS\n354\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"*\", AllowCredentials: false}\n355\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n356\t//\t    // Wildcard CORS is properly configured\n357\t//\t}\n358\t//\n359\t//\t// Validate full CORS configuration\n360\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{\n361\t//\t    AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\",\n362\t//\t    AllowMethods: \"GET, POST, OPTIONS\",\n363\t//\t    AllowHeaders: \"Content-Type, Authorization\",\n364\t//\t    AllowCredentials: true,\n365\t//\t}\n366\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n367\t//\t    // Full CORS configuration is valid\n368\t//\t}\n369\tfunc CORSHeadersIsValid(resp *http.Response, config *CORSConfig) bool {\n370\t\tif resp == nil {\n371\t\t\treturn false\n372\t\t}\n373\t\n374\t\t// If no config provided, do basic validation: check if Access-Control-Allow-Origin exists\n375\t\tif config == nil {\n376\t\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n377\t\t\treturn origin != \"\"\n378\t\t}\n379\t\n380\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Origin if specified\n381\t\tif config.AllowOrigin != \"\" {\n382\t\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n383\t\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n384\t\t\t\treturn false\n385\t\t\t}\n386\t\n387\t\t\t// Check for wildcard or exact match\n388\t\t\tif config.AllowOrigin == \"*\" {\n389\t\t\t\t// Wildcard is valid\n390\t\t\t\tif origin != \"*\" {\n391\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n392\t\t\t\t}\n393\t\t\t} else {\n394\t\t\t\t// Exact origin match (case-sensitive for origins)\n395\t\t\t\tif origin != config.AllowOrigin {\n396\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n397\t\t\t\t}\n398\t\t\t}\n399\t\t}\n400\t\n401\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Methods if specified\n402\t\tif config.AllowMethods != \"\" {\n403\t\t\tmethods := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n404\t\t\tif methods != config.AllowMethods {\n405\t\t\t\treturn false\n406\t\t\t}\n407\t\t}\n408\t\n409\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Headers if specified\n410\t\tif config.AllowHeaders != \"\" {\n411\t\t\theaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n412\t\t\tif headers != config.AllowHeaders {\n413\t\t\t\treturn false\n414\t\t\t}\n415\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Credentials if specified\n418\t\tif config.AllowCredentials {\n419\t\t\tcredentials := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Credentials\")\n420\t\t\tif credentials != \"true\" {\n421\t\t\t\treturn false\n422\t\t\t}\n423\t\t}\n424\t\n425\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Expose-Headers if specified\n426\t\tif config.ExposeHeaders != \"\" {\n427\t\t\texposeHeaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Expose-Headers\")\n428\t\t\tif exposeHeaders != config.ExposeHeaders {\n429\t\t\t\treturn false\n430\t\t\t}\n431\t\t}\n432\t\n433\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Max-Age if specified\n434\t\tif config.MaxAge != \"\" {\n435\t\t\tmaxAge := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Max-Age\")\n436\t\t\tif maxAge != config.MaxAge {\n437\t\t\t\treturn false\n438\t\t\t}\n439\t\t}\n440\t\n441\t\treturn true\n442\t}\n443\t\n444\t// =============================================================================\n445\t// ERROR MESSAGE PATTERN VALIDATION\n446\t// =============================================================================\n447\t\n448\t// ErrorMessagePattern specifies pattern matching configuration for error messages.\n449\ttype ErrorMessagePattern struct {\n450\t\t// Pattern is the regex pattern to match against error messages\n451\t\tPattern string\n452\t\n453\t\t// CaseInsensitive indicates whether matching should be case-insensitive\n454\t\tCaseInsensitive bool\n455\t\n456\t\t// MatchAny indicates whether to match any field (true) or all fields (false)\n457\t\tMatchAny bool\n458\t\n459\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\")\n460\t\t// If empty, checks default fields: \"error\", \"message\"\n461\t\tFieldNames []string\n462\t}\n463\t\n464\t// ValidateErrorMessagePattern validates error message content against regex patterns.\n465\t//\n466\t// This function extracts error messages from response bodies and validates them\n467\t// against a regex pattern. It supports both exact matching and case-insensitive\n468\t// pattern matching for flexible error validation.\n469\t//\n470\t// Parameters:\n471\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n472\t//   - pattern: Regex pattern to match against error messages\n473\t//   - caseInsensitive: If true, performs case-insensitive matching\n474\t//\n475\t// Returns:\n476\t//   - true if the error message matches the pattern, false otherwise\n477\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails or response is invalid\n478\t//\n479\t// Example usage:\n480\t//\n481\t//\t// Check for \"not found\" error (case-insensitive)\n482\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `(?i)not found`, false)\n483\t//\n484\t//\t// Check for \"invalid.*token\" pattern\n485\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `invalid.*token`, true)\n486\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePattern(bodyBytes []byte, pattern string, caseInsensitive bool) (bool, error) {\n487\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n488\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n489\t\t}\n490\t\n491\t\tif pattern == \"\" {\n492\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n493\t\t}\n494\t\n495\t\t// Parse JSON body\n496\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n497\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n498\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n499\t\t}\n500\t\n501\t\t// Compile regex pattern\n502\t\tregexPattern := pattern\n503\t\tif caseInsensitive {\n504\t\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + pattern\n505\t\t}\n506\t\n507\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n508\t\tif err != nil {\n509\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n510\t\t}\n511\t\n512\t\t// Check default error fields\n513\t\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n514\t\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n515\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n516\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n517\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n518\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n519\t\t\t\t\t}\n520\t\t\t\t}\n521\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n522\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n523\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n524\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok {\n525\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n526\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n527\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n528\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n529\t\t\t\t\t}\n530\t\t\t\t}\n531\t\t\t}\n532\t\t}\n533\t\n534\t\treturn false, nil\n535\t}\n536\t\n537\t// ValidateErrorMessage validates error message content against a pattern.\n538\t//\n539\t// This function checks if a response body contains an error message that matches\n540\t// the expected pattern. It supports both regex patterns and simple substring matching.\n541\t// When the pattern is found, it returns nil. When the pattern is not found, it returns\n542\t// a descriptive error including a snippet of the actual response.\n543\t//\n544\t// The function automatically detects whether the pattern is a regex or substring:\n545\t// - If the pattern contains regex metacharacters (. * + ? ^ $ { } [ ] ( ) | \\), it's treated as regex\n546\t// - Otherwise, it's treated as a simple substring search (case-sensitive)\n547\t//\n548\t// Parameters:\n549\t//   - response: Raw response body bytes\n550\t//   - expectedPattern: Pattern to search for (regex or substring)\n551\t//\n552\t// Returns:\n553\t//   - nil if the pattern is found in the response\n554\t//   - error if the pattern is not found or if response is invalid\n555\t//\n556\t// Example usage:\n557\t//\n558\t//\t// Regex pattern matching\n559\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*token\")\n560\t//\tif err != nil {\n561\t//\t    // Pattern not found\n562\t//\t}\n563\t//\n564\t//\t// Simple substring matching\n565\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"not found\")\n566\t//\tif err != nil {\n567\t//\t    // Substring not found\n568\t//\t}\n569\t//\n570\t//\t// Common error patterns\n571\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"unauthorized\")        // OAuth errors\n572\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*credentials\") // Auth errors\n573\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"rate.*limit\")          // Rate limiting\n574\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"timeout\")               // Timeout errors\n575\t//\n576\t// # Common Error Pattern Examples\n577\t//\n578\t// The function auto-detects regex vs substring matching:\n579\t//\n580\t// # OAuth 2.0 Errors\n581\t//\n582\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"error_description\": \"The access token expired\"}`)\n583\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid_token\")  // substring match\n584\t//\n585\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"access_denied\"}`)\n586\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"access_denied|invalid_grant\")  // regex match\n587\t//\n588\t// # Authentication Errors\n589\t//\n590\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Authentication failed: invalid credentials\"}`)\n591\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"authentication.*failed\")  // regex match\n592\t//\n593\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Invalid username or password\"}`)\n594\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*credentials\")  // regex match\n595\t//\n596\t// # Authorization Errors\n597\t//\n598\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Permission denied\"}`)\n599\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"permission.*denied\")  // regex match\n600\t//\n601\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"User is not authorized to access this resource\"}`)\n602\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not.*authorized\")  // regex match\n603\t//\n604\t// # Validation Errors\n605\t//\n606\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Validation failed: required field missing\"}`)\n607\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"validation.*failed\")  // regex match\n608\t//\n609\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"Invalid input: email format is incorrect\"}`)\n610\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*input\")  // regex match\n611\t//\n612\t// # Resource Errors\n613\t//\n614\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Resource not found\"}`)\n615\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not found\")  // substring match\n616\t//\n617\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"User does not exist\"}`)\n618\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"does not exist\")  // substring match\n619\t//\n620\t// # Rate Limiting Errors\n621\t//\n622\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Rate limit exceeded\"}`)\n623\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"rate.*limit\")  // regex match\n624\t//\n625\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"Too many requests, please try again later\"}`)\n626\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"too many requests\")  // substring match\n627\t//\n628\t// # Timeout Errors\n629\t//\n630\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Request timeout\"}`)\n631\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"timeout\")  // substring match\n632\t//\n633\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Connection timeout after 30 seconds\"}`)\n634\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"timeout\")  // substring match\n635\t//\n636\t// # Server Errors\n637\t//\n638\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Internal server error\"}`)\n639\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"internal.*error\")  // regex match\n640\t//\n641\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Service temporarily unavailable\"}`)\n642\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"unavailable\")  // substring match\n643\tfunc ValidateErrorMessage(response []byte, expectedPattern string) error {\n644\t\t// Validate inputs\n645\t\tif len(response) == 0 {\n646\t\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n647\t\t\t\tWithExpected(\"non-empty response\").\n648\t\t\t\tWithActual(\"\").\n649\t\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"cannot validate against empty response\").\n650\t\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n651\t\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n652\t\t\t\t\t\"Response body is empty\",\n653\t\t\t\t\t\"Cannot validate error message pattern against empty response\",\n654\t\t\t\t).\n655\t\t\t\tFormat()\n656\t\t}\n657\t\n658\t\tif expectedPattern == \"\" {\n659\t\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n660\t\t\t\tWithExpected(\"non-empty pattern\").\n661\t\t\t\tWithActual(\"\").\n662\t\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"pattern cannot be empty\").\n663\t\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n664\t\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n665\t\t\t\t\t\"Expected pattern cannot be empty\",\n666\t\t\t\t\t\"Provide a pattern to match against error messages\",\n667\t\t\t\t).\n668\t\t\t\tFormat()\n669\t\t}\n670\t\n671\t\t// Parse JSON body\n672\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n673\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(response, &body); err != nil {\n674\t\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n675\t\t\t\tWithExpected(\"valid JSON\").\n676\t\t\t\tWithActual(\"\").\n677\t\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"failed to parse response body\").\n678\t\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n679\t\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(fmt.Sprintf(\"Parse error: %v\", err)).\n680\t\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n681\t\t\t\t\t\"Failed to parse response body\",\n682\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Parse error: %v\", err),\n683\t\t\t\t\t\"Ensure response contains valid JSON\",\n684\t\t\t\t).\n685\t\t\t\tFormat()\n686\t\t}\n687\t\n688\t\t// Search for error messages in common fields\n689\t\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\", \"error_description\"}\n690\t\ttype foundMessage struct {\n691\t\t\tmessage   string\n692\t\t\tfieldName string\n693\t\t}\n694\t\tvar foundMessages []foundMessage\n695\t\n696\t\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n697\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n698\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n699\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, foundMessage{\n700\t\t\t\t\t\tmessage:   strValue,\n701\t\t\t\t\t\tfieldName: field,\n702\t\t\t\t\t})\n703\t\t\t\t}\n704\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n705\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n706\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n707\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n708\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, foundMessage{\n709\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmessage:   strValue,\n710\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfieldName: field + \".message\",\n711\t\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n712\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n713\t\t\t\t\t}\n714\t\t\t\t}\n715\t\t\t}\n716\t\t}\n717\t\n718\t\t// If no error messages found, return detailed error with response snippet\n719\t\tif len(foundMessages) == 0 {\n720\t\t\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\n721\t\t\tresponseSize := len(response)\n722\t\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n723\t\t\t\tWithExpected(\"error message field\").\n724\t\t\t\tWithActual(\"\").\n725\t\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"no error message found in response\").\n726\t\t\t\tWithResponseSnippet(snippet).\n727\t\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n728\t\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(\n729\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Checked fields: %s\", strings.Join(defaultFields, \", \")),\n730\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Response size: %d bytes\", responseSize),\n731\t\t\t\t).\n732\t\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n733\t\t\t\t\t\"No error message found in response body\",\n734\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Checked fields: %s\", strings.Join(defaultFields, \", \")),\n735\t\t\t\t\t\"Response may not contain standard error fields\",\n736\t\t\t\t).\n737\t\t\t\tFormat()\n738\t\t}\n739\t\n740\t\t// Detect if pattern is regex or substring\n741\t\tisRegex := containsRegexMetacharacters(expectedPattern)\n742\t\tpatternType := \"substring\"\n743\t\tif isRegex {\n744\t\t\tpatternType = \"regex\"\n745\t\t}\n746\t\n747\t\t// Check each found message for pattern match\n748\t\tfor _, foundMsg := range foundMessages {\n749\t\t\tvar matched bool\n750\t\n751\t\t\tif isRegex {\n752\t\t\t\t// Try regex matching\n753\t\t\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(expectedPattern)\n754\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n755\t\t\t\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n756\t\t\t\t\t\t\"error_message\",\n757\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"valid regex pattern '%s'\", expectedPattern),\n758\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid regex '%s'\", expectedPattern),\n759\t\t\t\t\t\t\"\",\n760\t\t\t\t\t\t\"\",\n761\t\t\t\t\t\tfoundMsg.fieldName,\n762\t\t\t\t\t\t\"\",\n763\t\t\t\t\t\tnil,\n764\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern type: regex (contains metacharacters), Parse error: %v\", err),\n765\t\t\t\t\t\t\"\",\n766\t\t\t\t\t\t[]string{\n767\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid regex pattern: %s\", expectedPattern),\n768\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Regex compilation error: %v\", err),\n769\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Check regex syntax and escape special characters\",\n770\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n771\t\t\t\t\t)\n772\t\t\t\t}\n773\t\t\t\tmatched = re.MatchString(foundMsg.message)\n774\t\t\t} else {\n775\t\t\t\t// Simple substring matching (case-sensitive)\n776\t\t\t\tmatched = strings.Contains(foundMsg.message, expectedPattern)\n777\t\t\t}\n778\t\n779\t\t\tif matched {\n780\t\t\t\t// Pattern found - return nil (success)\n781\t\t\t\treturn nil\n782\t\t\t}\n783\t\t}\n784\t\n785\t\t// Pattern not found in any messages - return descriptive error with full details\n786\t\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\n787\t\tfirstMessage := foundMessages[0]\n788\t\n789\t\t// Build validation details including response size context\n790\t\tresponseSize := len(response)\n791\t\tvar validationDetails []string\n792\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern type: %s\", patternType))\n793\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected pattern: %s\", expectedPattern))\n794\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Actual error message: \\\"%s\\\"\", firstMessage.message))\n795\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Field name: %s\", firstMessage.fieldName))\n796\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Checked fields: %s\", strings.Join(defaultFields, \", \")))\n797\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Response size: %d bytes\", responseSize))\n798\t\n799\t\tif len(foundMessages) > 1 {\n800\t\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Found %d error message fields in response\", len(foundMessages)))\n801\t\t}\n802\t\n803\t\t// Generate pattern-specific suggestions\n804\t\tsuggestions := generatePatternMismatchSuggestions(expectedPattern, firstMessage.message, isRegex)\n805\t\n806\t\t// Use ValidationFormatter builder pattern for enhanced error formatting\n807\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n808\t\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n809\t\t\tWithActual(firstMessage.message).\n810\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s pattern '%s' did not match\", patternType, expectedPattern)).\n811\t\t\tWithResponseSnippet(snippet).\n812\t\t\tWithFieldName(firstMessage.fieldName).\n813\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n814\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(validationDetails...).\n815\t\t\tWithSuggestions(suggestions...).\n816\t\t\tFormat()\n817\t}\n818\t\n819\t// containsRegexMetacharacters checks if a string contains regex metacharacters.\n820\t// This is used to auto-detect whether a pattern should be treated as regex or substring.\n821\tfunc containsRegexMetacharacters(pattern string) bool {\n822\t\tregexChars := []string{\".\", \"*\", \"+\", \"?\", \"^\", \"$\", \"{\", \"}\", \"[\", \"]\", \"(\", \")\", \"|\", \"\\\\\"}\n823\t\tfor _, char := range regexChars {\n824\t\t\tif strings.Contains(pattern, char) {\n825\t\t\t\treturn true\n826\t\t\t}\n827\t\t}\n828\t\treturn false\n829\t}\n830\t\n831\t// generatePatternMismatchSuggestions provides relevant suggestions based on pattern and actual message.\n832\t// This function analyzes the expected pattern and actual message to generate specific suggestions.\n833\tfunc generatePatternMismatchSuggestions(expectedPattern, actualMessage string, isRegex bool) []string {\n834\t\tvar suggestions []string\n835\t\n836\t\texpectedLower := strings.ToLower(expectedPattern)\n837\t\tactualLower := strings.ToLower(actualMessage)\n838\t\n839\t\t// Common pattern mismatch checks\n840\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"token\") && !strings.Contains(actualLower, \"token\") {\n841\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n842\t\t\t\t\"Pattern looks for 'token' but actual message doesn't contain it\",\n843\t\t\t\t\"Check if the error is about authentication/authorization rather than tokens\",\n844\t\t\t\t\"Consider expanding pattern to include 'auth' or 'access' related terms\",\n845\t\t\t)\n846\t\t}\n847\t\n848\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"invalid\") && !strings.Contains(actualLower, \"invalid\") {\n849\t\t\tif strings.Contains(actualLower, \"expired\") {\n850\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n851\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern expects 'invalid' but message contains 'expired'\",\n852\t\t\t\t\t\"Consider using pattern: 'invalid.*expired|expired.*invalid' to match both cases\",\n853\t\t\t\t\t\"Or use separate patterns for different token states\",\n854\t\t\t\t)\n855\t\t\t}\n856\t\t}\n857\t\n858\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"not found\") && strings.Contains(actualLower, \"does not exist\") {\n859\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n860\t\t\t\t\"Pattern uses 'not found' but message uses 'does not exist'\",\n861\t\t\t\t\"Consider using pattern: 'not found|does not exist' to match both phrasings\",\n862\t\t\t\t\"Or use simpler pattern: 'not.*found|does.*not.*exist'\",\n863\t\t\t)\n864\t\t}\n865\t\n866\t\tif isRegex {\n867\t\t\t// Regex-specific suggestions\n868\t\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \".\") && !strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \"\\\\\") {\n869\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n870\t\t\t\t\t\"Regex contains '.' which matches any character (except newline)\",\n871\t\t\t\t\t\"If you meant literal dot, escape it: '\\\\.'\",\n872\t\t\t\t\t\"If substring search is intended, consider using plain text without metacharacters\",\n873\t\t\t\t)\n874\t\t\t}\n875\t\n876\t\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \"*\") && !strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \".*\") {\n877\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n878\t\t\t\t\t\"Regex contains bare '*' which is a quantifier, not a wildcard\",\n879\t\t\t\t\t\"For 'any characters' pattern, use: '.*' instead of '*'\",\n880\t\t\t\t\t\"For 'zero or more' of previous character, ensure '*' is preceded by pattern to repeat\",\n881\t\t\t\t)\n882\t\t\t}\n883\t\n884\t\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \"^\") || strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \"$\") {\n885\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n886\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern uses anchors (^ or $) which match start/end of string\",\n887\t\t\t\t\t\"Ensure actual message doesn't have extra whitespace or newlines\",\n888\t\t\t\t\t\"Consider removing anchors if message may contain additional text\",\n889\t\t\t\t)\n890\t\t\t}\n891\t\n892\t\t\t// Case sensitivity check - detect if pattern and message have different casing\n893\t\t\texpectedHasMixedCase := expectedPattern != expectedLower\n894\t\t\tactualHasMixedCase := actualMessage != actualLower\n895\t\n896\t\t\t// Check if there's a case mismatch between pattern and message\n897\t\t\tif expectedHasMixedCase && actualHasMixedCase && expectedLower != actualLower {\n898\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n899\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern is case-sensitive but may need case-insensitive matching\",\n900\t\t\t\t\t\"Consider using '(?i)' prefix for case-insensitive regex\",\n901\t\t\t\t\t\"Or convert pattern to handle different casing patterns\",\n902\t\t\t\t)\n903\t\t\t}\n904\t\t} else {\n905\t\t\t// Substring-specific suggestions\n906\t\t\tif expectedLower != actualLower && strings.Contains(actualLower, expectedLower) {\n907\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n908\t\t\t\t\t\"Substring match is case-sensitive\",\n909\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern was found but with different casing\",\n910\t\t\t\t\t\"Consider using regex with '(?i)' prefix for case-insensitive matching\",\n911\t\t\t\t)\n912\t\t\t}\n913\t\n914\t\t\t// Check for word boundary issues\n915\t\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \" \") && strings.Contains(actualMessage, strings.ReplaceAll(expectedPattern, \" \", \"\")) {\n916\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n917\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern contains spaces but actual message may not\",\n918\t\t\t\t\t\"Consider using regex with '\\\\s*' to match optional whitespace\",\n919\t\t\t\t\t\"Or remove spaces from pattern for flexible matching\",\n920\t\t\t\t)\n921\t\t\t}\n922\t\t}\n923\t\n924\t\t// Pattern complexity suggestions\n925\t\tif len(expectedPattern) > 50 {\n926\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n927\t\t\t\t\"Pattern is quite long and complex\",\n928\t\t\t\t\"Consider breaking it into simpler patterns\",\n929\t\t\t\t\"Test pattern incrementally to identify which part fails\",\n930\t\t\t)\n931\t\t}\n932\t\n933\t\t// If no specific suggestions, provide generic ones\n934\t\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n935\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n936\t\t\t\t\"Review the expected pattern and actual message content\",\n937\t\t\t\t\"Check if pattern should be regex or substring matching\",\n938\t\t\t\t\"Verify the actual message format matches expectations\",\n939\t\t\t\t\"Consider using a more flexible pattern if multiple message formats are possible\",\n940\t\t\t)\n941\t\t}\n942\t\n943\t\treturn suggestions\n944\t}\n945\t\n946\t// extractResponseSnippet creates a truncated snippet of the response body for error messages.\n947\t// It limits the snippet to 200 characters to keep error messages readable.\n948\tfunc extractResponseSnippet(response []byte) string {\n949\t\t// Convert to string and truncate\n950\t\tresponseStr := string(response)\n951\t\tmaxLen := 200\n952\t\tif len(responseStr) > maxLen {\n953\t\t\tresponseStr = responseStr[:maxLen] + \"...\"\n954\t\t}\n955\t\t// Replace newlines with spaces for single-line error message\n956\t\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \"\\n\", \" \")\n957\t\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \"\\r\", \" \")\n958\t\treturn responseStr\n959\t}\n960\t\n961\t// ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig validates error messages using advanced pattern configuration.\n962\t//\n963\t// This function provides flexible error message validation with support for:\n964\t// - Custom field selection\n965\t// - Case-insensitive matching\n966\t// - Match-any vs match-all semantics\n967\t// - Multiple pattern checking\n968\t//\n969\t// Parameters:\n970\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n971\t//   - config: Pattern matching configuration\n972\t//\n973\t// Returns:\n974\t//   - true if validation succeeds, false otherwise\n975\t//   - error if configuration is invalid or JSON parsing fails\n976\t//\n977\t// Example usage:\n978\t//\n979\t//\tconfig := &ErrorMessagePattern{\n980\t//\t    Pattern:         \"unauthorized\",\n981\t//\t    CaseInsensitive: true,\n982\t//\t    FieldNames:      []string{\"error\", \"detail\"},\n983\t//\t}\n984\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(body, config)\n985\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(bodyBytes []byte, config *ErrorMessagePattern) (bool, error) {\n986\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n987\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n988\t\t}\n989\t\n990\t\tif config == nil {\n991\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"config cannot be nil\")\n992\t\t}\n993\t\n994\t\tif config.Pattern == \"\" {\n995\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n996\t\t}\n997\t\n998\t\t// Parse JSON body\n999\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n1000\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n1001\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n1002\t\t}\n1003\t\n1004\t\t// Compile regex pattern\n1005\t\tregexPattern := config.Pattern\n1006\t\tif config.CaseInsensitive {\n1007\t\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + config.Pattern\n1008\t\t}\n1009\t\n1010\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n1011\t\tif err != nil {\n1012\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n1013\t\t}\n1014\t\n1015\t\t// Determine which fields to check\n1016\t\tfieldsToCheck := config.FieldNames\n1017\t\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\n1018\t\t\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n1019\t\t}\n1020\t\n1021\t\t// Check each specified field\n1022\t\tmatchedFields := []string{}\n1023\t\tfor _, field := range fieldsToCheck {\n1024\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n1025\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1026\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n1027\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\n1028\t\t\t\t\t\tif config.MatchAny {\n1029\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n1030\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1031\t\t\t\t\t}\n1032\t\t\t\t}\n1033\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n1034\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n1035\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n1036\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1037\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n1038\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\n1039\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif config.MatchAny {\n1040\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n1041\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1042\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1043\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1044\t\t\t\t\t}\n1045\t\t\t\t}\n1046\t\t\t}\n1047\t\t}\n1048\t\n1049\t\t// For match-all semantics, require all specified fields to match\n1050\t\tif !config.MatchAny {\n1051\t\t\treturn len(matchedFields) == len(fieldsToCheck), nil\n1052\t\t}\n1053\t\n1054\t\treturn false, nil\n1055\t}\n1056\t\n1057\t// =============================================================================\n1058\t// SPECIFIC ERROR CODE VALIDATION\n1059\t// =============================================================================\n1060\t\n1061\t// ErrorCodeInResponse checks if a response body contains a specific error code.\n1062\t//\n1063\t// This function searches for error codes in common error response fields.\n1064\t// It supports both simple string error codes and nested error objects.\n1065\t//\n1066\t// Parameters:\n1067\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n1068\t//   - expectedCode: The error code to look for (e.g., \"INVALID_TOKEN\", \"AUTH_FAILED\")\n1069\t//\n1070\t// Returns:\n1071\t//   - true if the error code is found in the response, false otherwise\n1072\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n1073\t//\n1074\t// Example usage:\n1075\t//\n1076\t//\t// Check for \"AUTH_FAILED\" error code\n1077\t//\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"AUTH_FAILED\")\n1078\t//\n1079\t//\t// Check for OAuth \"invalid_token\" error\n1080\t//\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"invalid_token\")\n1081\tfunc ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes []byte, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\n1082\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n1083\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n1084\t\t}\n1085\t\n1086\t\tif expectedCode == \"\" {\n1087\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"expected code cannot be empty\")\n1088\t\t}\n1089\t\n1090\t\t// Parse JSON body\n1091\t\tvar body Common error code field names\n1097\t\terrorCodeFields := []string{\"error_code\", \"errorCode\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"}\n1098\t\n1099\t\t// Check for error code in standard fields\n1100\t\tfor _, field := range errorCodeFields {\n1101\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n1102\t\t\t\t// Check for string match\n1103\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n1104\t\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n1105\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n1106\t\t\t\t\t}\n1107\t\t\t\t}\n1108\t\t\t\t// Check for nested error code in error codeValue.(string); ok {\n1112\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n1113\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n1114\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1115\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1116\t\t\t\t\t}\n1117\t\t\t\t}\n1118\t\t\t}\n1119\t\t}\n1120\t\n1121\t\t// Check for OAuth-style error field (contains error code directly)\n1122\t\tif errorCode, exists := body[\"error\"]; exists {\n1123\t\t\tif strValue, ok := errorCode.(string); ok {\n1124\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n1125\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n1126\t\t\t\t}\n1127\t\t\t}\n1128\t\t}\n1129\t\n1130\t\treturn false, nil\n1131\t}\n1132\t\n1133\t// ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode validates both status code and error code in a single check.\n1134\t//\n1135\t// This is a convenience function that validates HTTP status code and error code\n1136\t// together, ensuring that both the HTTP layer and application layer error\n1137\t// information are consistent.\n1138\t//\n1139\t// Parameters:\n1140\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1141\t//   - expectedStatus: Expected HTTP status code\n1142\t//   - expectedCode: ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp *http.Response, expectedStatus int, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\n1156\t\tif resp == nil {\n1157\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response is nil\")\n1158\t\t}\n1159\t\n1160\t\t// Check status code\n1161\t\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatus {\n1162\t\t\treturn false, nil\n1163\t\t}\n1164\t\n1165\t\t// Read response body\n1166\t\tvar {\n1172\t\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", err)\n1173\t\t\t}\n1174\t\t}\n1175\t\n1176\t\t// Check error code\n1177\t\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes, expectedCode)\n1178\t\tif err != nil {\n1179\t\t\treturn false, err\n1180\t\t}\n1181\t\n1182\t\treturn found, nil\n1183\t}\n1184\t\n1185\t// GetErrorMessage extracts the error message from a response GetErrorMessage(bodyBytes []byte) (string, error) {\n1204\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n1205\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n1206\t\t}\n1207\t\n1208\t\t// Parse JSON err)\n1212\t\t}\n1213\t\n1214\t\t// Check common message fields\n1215\t\tmessageFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\", \"error_description\"}\n1216\t\tfor _, field := nil\n1284\t}\n1285\t\n1286\t// =============================================================================\n1287\t// DETAILED VALIDATION RESULTS\n1288\t// =============================================================================\n1289\t\n1290\t// StatusCodeValidationResult provides detailed information about status code validation.\n1291\t// It includes not just whether validation passed, but also the expected vs actual values\n1292\t// and detailed mismatch information to help diagnose validation failures.\n1293\ttype StatusCodeValidationResult struct {\n1294\t\t// Valid indicates whether the validation passed\n1295\t\tValid bool\n1296\t\t// ActualCode is the HTTP status code from the response\n1297\t\tActualCode int\n1298\t\t// ExpectedCodes contains the expected status code(s) that were validated against\n1299\t\tExpectedCodes []int\n1300\t\t// MatchedCode is the specific expected code that matched (if any)\n1301\t\tMatchedCode *int\n1302\t\t// MismatchDetails contains human-readable information about why validation failed\n1303\t\tMismatchDetails string\n1304\t\t// IsClientError indicates whether the actual code is a client error (4xx)\n1305\t\tIsClientError bool\n1306\t\t// IsServerError indicates whether the actual code is a server error (5xx)\n1307\t\tIsServerError bool\n1308\t\t// Category describes the general category of the actual status code\n1309\t\tCategory string\n1310\t}\n1311\t\n1312\t// ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails performs comprehensive status code validation with detailed results.\n1313\t// Unlike HTTPStatusCodeIsValid which returns only a boolean, this function provides detailed information\n1314\t// about the validation process, making it easier to diagnose why validation failed.\n1315\t//\n1316\t// Parameters:\n1317\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1318\t//   - expected: Expected status code(s) - can be a single int or []int\n1319\t//   - requestContext: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"POST /api/users\", \"GET /api/resource/123\")\n1320\t//\n1321\t// Returns a StatusCodeValidationResult with detailed validation information.\n1322\t//\n1323\t// Example usage:\n1324\t//\n1325\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(response, []int{200, 201, 204}, \"POST /api/users\")\n1326\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1327\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status validation failed: %s\", result.MismatchDetails)\n1328\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Got: %d, expected one of: %v\", result.ActualCode, result.ExpectedCodes)\n1329\t//\t}\n1330\t//\n1331\t//\t// Single code validation without context\n1332\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(response, 200, \"\")\n1333\t//\tif result.Valid {\n1334\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status code is valid: %d\", result.ActualCode)\n1335\t//\t}\n1336\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}, requestContext ...string) StatusCodeValidationResult {\n1337\t\tresult := StatusCodeValidationResult{\n1338\t\t\tValid:             false,\n1339\t\t\tActualCode:        0,\n1340\t\t\tExpectedCodes:     []int{},\n1341\t\t\tMatchedCode:       nil,\n1342\t\t\tMismatchDetails:   \"\",\n1343\t\t\tIsClientError:     false,\n1344\t\t\tIsServerError:     false,\n1345\t\t\tCategory:          \"unknown\",\n1346\t\t}\n1347\t\n1348\t\tif resp == nil {\n1349\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"response is nil\"\n1350\t\t\treturn result\n1351\t\t}\n1352\t\n1353\t\tresult.ActualCode = resp.StatusCode\n1354\t\n1355\t\t// Set error flags and category\n1356\t\tswitch {\n1357\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500:\n1358\t\t\tresult.IsClientError = true\n1359\t\t\tresult.Category = \"client_error\"\n1360\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600:\n1361\t\t\tresult.IsServerError = true\n1362\t\t\tresult.Category = \"server_error\"\n1363\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 200 && resp.StatusCode < 300:\n1364\t\t\tresult.Category = \"success\"\n1365\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 300 && resp.StatusCode < 400:\n1366\t\t\tresult.Category = \"redirection\"\n1367\t\tdefault:\n1368\t\t\tresult.Category = \"other\"\n1369\t\t}\n1370\t\n1371\t\t// Parse expected codes\n1372\t\tvar expectedCodes []int\n1373\t\tswitch v := expected.(type) {\n1374\t\tcase int:\n1375\t\t\texpectedCodes = []int{v}\n1376\t\tcase []int:\n1377\t\t\texpectedCodes = v\n1378\t\tdefault:\n1379\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid expected type: %T\", expected)\n1380\t\t\treturn result\n1381\t\t}\n1382\t\n1383\t\tresult.ExpectedCodes = expectedCodes\n1384\t\n1385\t\t// Check for match\n1386\t\tfor _, code := range expectedCodes {\n1387\t\t\tif resp.StatusCode == code {\n1388\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = true\n1389\t\t\t\tresult.MatchedCode = &code\n1390\t\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"\"\n1391\t\t\t\treturn result\n1392\t\t\t}\n1393\t\t}\n1394\t\n1395\t\t// Extract optional context\n1396\t\tcontext := \"\"\n1397\t\tif len(requestContext) > 0 && requestContext[0] != \"\" {\n1398\t\t\tcontext = requestContext[0]\n1399\t\t}\n1400\t\n1401\t\t// Build comprehensive mismatch information with suggestions\n1402\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = buildComprehensiveMismatchDetails(\n1403\t\t\texpectedCodes, resp.StatusCode, result.Category, result.IsClientError, result.IsServerError, context)\n1404\t\n1405\t\treturn result\n1406\t}\n1407\t\n1408\t// buildComprehensiveMismatchDetails creates detailed mismatch information including\n1409\t// status code category, context, and specific suggestions based on the actual code.\n1410\t// It uses FormatValidationErrorWithDetails for consistent formatting across the codebase.\n1411\tfunc buildComprehensiveMismatchDetails(expectedCodes []int, actualCode int, category string, isClientError, isServerError bool, context string) string {\n1412\t\t// Build validation details that include status code category information\n1413\t\tvar validationDetails []string\n1414\t\n1415\t\t// Add status code category information to validation details\n1416\t\tcategoryDesc := getCategoryDescription(category)\n1417\t\tif categoryDesc != \"\" {\n1418\t\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code category: %s\", categoryDesc))\n1419\t\t}\n1420\t\n1421\t\t// Prepare range info with category details\n1422\t\tvar rangeInfo string\n1423\t\tif isClientError {\n1424\t\t\trangeInfo = \"400-499 (Client Error)\"\n1425\t\t} else if isServerError {\n1426\t\t\trangeInfo = \"500-599 (Server Error)\"\n1427\t\t} else if category == \"success\" {\n1428\t\t\trangeInfo = \"200-299 (Success)\"\n1429\t\t} else if category == \"redirection\" {\n1430\t\t\trangeInfo = \"300-399 (Redirection)\"\n1431\t\t}\n1432\t\n1433\t\t// Determine expected value for FormatValidationErrorWithDetails\n1434\t\tvar expected interface{}\n1435\t\tif len(expectedCodes) == 1 {\n1436\t\t\texpected = expectedCodes[0]\n1437\t\t} else {\n1438\t\t\texpected = expectedCodes\n1439\t\t}\n1440\t\n1441\t\t// Use FormatValidationErrorWithDetails for consistent error formatting\n1442\t\tvalidationErr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n1443\t\t\t\"status_code\",\n1444\t\t\texpected,\n1445\t\t\tactualCode,\n1446\t\t\tcontext,\n1447\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet - not needed for status code validation\n1448\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName - not applicable for status code validation\n1449\t\t\t\"\", // location - not applicable for status code validation\n1450\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields - not applicable for status code validation\n1451\t\t\t\"\", // patternDetails - not applicable for status code validation\n1452\t\t\trangeInfo,\n1453\t\t\tvalidationDetails,\n1454\t\t)\n1455\t\n1456\t\t// Return the formatted error string\n1457\t\treturn validationErr.Error()\n1458\t}\n1459\t\n1460\t// getCategoryDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code category.\n1461\tfunc getCategoryDescription(category string) string {\n1462\t\tdescriptions := map[string]string{\n1463\t\t\t\"success\":       \"2xx - Successful responses\",\n1464\t\t\t\"redirection\":   \"3xx - Redirection messages\",\n1465\t\t\t\"client_error\":  \"4xx - Client error responses\",\n1466\t\t\t\"server_error\":  \"5xx - Server error responses\",\n1467\t\t\t\"other\":         \"Non-standard status codes\",\n1468\t\t}\n1469\t\treturn descriptions[category]\n1470\t}\n1471\t\n1472\t// getStatusCodeSpecificSuggestions returns relevant suggestions based on the specific status code received.\n1473\tfunc getStatusCodeSpecificSuggestions(code int) []string {\n1474\t\tswitch code {\n1475\t\tcase 400:\n1476\t\t\treturn []string{\n1477\t\t\t\t\"Check request body syntax and formatting\",\n1478\t\t\t\t\"Verify all required fields are present\",\n1479\t\t\t\t\"Ensure Content-Type header matches request body format\",\n1480\t\t\t\t\"Validate JSON schema if expired\",\n1486\t\t\t\t\"Ensure Authorization header is properly formatted (e.g., 'Bearer <token>')\",\n1487\t\t\t\t\"Confirm authentication method is supported\",\n1488\t\t\t}\n1489\t\tcase API documentation for required permissions\",\n1494\t\t\t\t\"Ensure proper OAuth scopes are granted\",\n1495\t\t\t}\n1496\t\tcase 404:\n1497\t\t\treturn []string{\n1498\t\t\t\t\"Verify the endpoint URL is correct\",\n1499\t\t\t\t\"Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\",\n1500\t\t\t\t\"Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\",\n1501\t\t\t\t\"Review API versioning (URL path may []string{\n1533\t\t\t\t\"Implement retry with exponential backoff\",\n1534\t\t\t\t\"Check service maintenance schedules\",\n1535\t\t\t\t\"Verify service capacity and scaling\",\n1536\t\t\t\t\"Review slowly\",\n1542\t\t\t\t\"Review request complexity and data size\",\n1543\t\t\t\t\"Verify network latency and connectivity\",\n1544\t\t\t}\n1545\t\tdefault:\n1546\t\t\t// Generic suggestions for other codes\n1547\t\t\tif code >= 400 && code < 500 {\n1548\t\t\t\treturn []string{\n1549\t\t\t\t\t\"Review request parameters and body\",\n1550\t\t\t\t\t\"Check API documentation for requirements\",\n1551\t\t\t\t\t\"Verify all headers are correctly set\",\n1552\t\t\t\t\t\"Ensure request complies with API contracts\",\n1553\t\t\t\t}\n1554\t\t\t}\n1555\t\t\tif code >= 500 && code < 600 {\n1556\t\t\t\treturn []string{\n1557\t\t\t\t\t\"Implement retry logic with exponential backoff\",\n1558\t\t\t\t\t\"Check service status page for ongoing issues\",\n1559\t\t\t\t\t\"Contact support if the issue persists\",\n1560\t\t\t\t\t\"Review server logs if accessible\",\n1561\t\t\t\t}\n1562\t\t\t}\n1563\t\t}\n1564\t\treturn []string{}\n1565\t}\n1566\t\n1567\t// StatusCodeRange defines a range of status codes for flexible validation.\n1568\t// This is useful when you want to accept any status code within a certain range.\n1569\ttype StatusCodeRange struct {\n1570\t\t// Min is the minimum status code in the range (inclusive)\n1571\t\tMin int\n1572\t\t// Max is the maximum status code in the range (inclusive)\n1573\t\tMax int\n1574\t\t// Description is a human-readable description of what this range represents\n1575\t\tDescription string\n1576\t}\n1577\t\n1578\t// Common status code ranges\n1579\tvar (\n1580\t\t// Range1xx covers informational responses (100-199)\n1581\t\tRange1xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 100, Max: 199, Description: \"Informational\"}\n1582\t\t// Range2xx covers successful responses (200-299)\n1583\t\tRange2xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 299, Description: \"Success\"}\n1584\t\t// Range3xx covers redirection responses (300-399)\n1585\t\tRange3xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 300, Max: 399, Description: \"Redirection\"}\n1586\t\t// Range4xx covers client error responses (400-499)\n1587\t\tRange4xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 400, Max: 499, Description: \"Client Error\"}\n1588\t\t// Range5xx covers server error responses (500-599)\n1589\t\tRange5xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 500, Max: 599, Description: \"Server Error\"}\n1590\t)\n1591\t\n1592\t// ValidateStatusCodeRange checks if a response's status code falls within a specified range.\n1593\t// This is useful for flexible validation where you want to accept any status code within a category.\n1594\t//\n1595\t// Parameters:\n1596\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1597\t//   - range: The status code range to validate against\n1598\t//\n1599\t// Returns true if the response status code falls within the range, false otherwise.\n1600\t// Returns false if the response is nil or the range is invalid (Min > Max).\n1601\t//\n1602\t// Example usage:\n1603\t//\n1604\t//\t// Accept any success code (2xx)\n1605\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, Range2xx) {\n1606\t//\t    // Response is successful (200-299)\n1607\t//\t}\n1608\t//\n1609\t//\t// Accept any client error (4xx)\n1610\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, Range4xx) {\n1611\t//\t    // Response is a client error (400-499)\n1612\t//\t}\n1613\t//\n1614\t//\t// Custom range for specific validation\n1615\t//\tcustomRange := StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 204, Description: \"Standard success\"}\n1616\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, customRange) {\n1617\t//\t    // Response is in custom range\n1618\t//\t}\n1619\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRange(resp *http.Response, statusRange StatusCodeRange) bool {\n1620\t\tif resp == nil {\n1621\t\t\treturn false\n1622\t\t}\n1623\t\n1624\t\tif statusRange.Min > statusRange.Max {\n1625\t\t\treturn false\n1626\t\t}\n1627\t\n1628\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= statusRange.Min && resp.StatusCode <= statusRange.Max\n1629\t}\n1630\t\n1631\t// ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails provides detailed status code range validation.\n1632\t// Like ValidateStatusCodeRange but with comprehensive result information including\n1633\t// range boundaries, distance from valid range, and contextual suggestions.\n1634\t//\n1635\t// Parameters:\n1636\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1637\t//   - range: The status code range to validate against\n1638\t//\n1639\t// Returns a context (e.g., '4xx', '5xx'), and suggestions for fixing the issue.\n1642\t//\n1643\t// Example usage:\n1644\t//\n1645\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails(response, Range4xx)\n1646\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1647\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status code %d is not in range %s (%d-%d)\",\n1648\t//\t        result.ActualCode, result.Category, range.Min, range.Max)\n1649\t//\t    // MismatchDetails now includes:\n1650\t//\t    // - Expected range with description (e.g., \"400-499 Client Error\")\n1651\t//\t    // - Actual received status code\n1652\t//\t    // - Distance from valid range (e.g., \"99 codes below range\")\n1653\t//\t    // - Range pattern context (e.g., \"Validating 4xx pattern\")\n1654\t//\t    // - Specific suggestions based on the actual code\n1655\t//\t}\n1656\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails(resp *http.Response, statusRange StatusCodeRange) StatusCodeValidationResult {\n1657\t\tresult := StatusCodeValidationResult{\n1658\t\t\tValid:           false,\n1659\t\t\tActualCode:      0,\n1660\t\t\tExpectedCodes:   []int{statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max},\n1661\t\t\tMatchedCode:     nil,\n1662\t\t\tMismatchDetails: \"\",\n1663\t\t\tIsClientError:   false,\n1664\t\t\tIsServerError:   false,\n1665\t\t\tCategory:        statusRange.Description,\n1666\t\t}\n1667\t\n1668\t\tif resp == nil {\n1669\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"response is nil\"\n1670\t\t\treturn result\n1671\t\t}\n1672\t\n1673\t\tif statusRange.Min > statusRange.Max {\n1674\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid range: Min (%d) > Max (%d)\", statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max)\n1675\t\t\treturn result\n1676\t\t}\n1677\t\n1678\t\tresult.ActualCode = resp.StatusCode\n1679\t\n1680\t\t// Check range\n1681\t\tinRange := resp.StatusCode >= statusRange.Min && resp.StatusCode <= statusRange.Max\n1682\t\tresult.Valid = inRange\n1683\t\n1684\t\t// Set error flags\n1685\t\tif resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500 {\n1686\t\t\tresult.IsClientError = true\n1687\t\t}\n1688\t\tif resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600 {\n1689\t\t\tresult.IsServerError = true\n1690\t\t}\n1691\t\n1692\t\tif !inRange {\n1693\t\t\t// Build comprehensive mismatch details with range boundaries, distance, and suggestions\n1694\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = buildRangeMismatchDetails(resp.StatusCode, statusRange)\n1695\t\t}\n1696\t\n1697\t\treturn result\n1698\t}\n1699\t\n1700\t// buildRangeMismatchDetails creates comprehensive mismatch information for range validation failures.\n1701\t// This function calculates the distance from the valid range, identifies the range pattern context,\n1702\t// and generates detailed error messages with suggestions using FormatValidationErrorWithDetails.\n1703\tfunc buildRangeMismatchDetails(actualCode int, statusRange StatusCodeRange) string {\n1704\t\t// Calculate distance from valid range\n1705\t\tvar distanceInfo string\n1706\t\tvar distanceDetails []string\n1707\t\n1708\t\tif actualCode < statusRange.Min {\n1709\t\t\tdistance := statusRange.Min - actualCode\n1710\t\t\tif distance == 1 {\n1711\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d code below range\", distance)\n1712\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d code below the minimum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Min))\n1713\t\t\t} else {\n1714\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d codes below range\", distance)\n1715\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d codes below the minimum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Min))\n1716\t\t\t}\n1717\t\t} else {\n1718\t\t\tdistance := actualCode - statusRange.Max\n1719\t\t\tif distance == 1 {\n1720\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d code above range\", distance)\n1721\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d code above the maximum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Max))\n1722\t\t\t} else {\n1723\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d codes above range\", distance)\n1724\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d codes above the maximum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Max))\n1725\t\t\t}\n1726\t\t}\n1727\t\n1728\t\t// Determine range pattern context (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n1729\t\trangePattern := getRangePattern(statusRange)\n1730\t\n1731\t\t// Build expected range with description\n1732\t\texpectedRange := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max)\n1733\t\tif statusRange.Description != \"\" {\n1734\t\t\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", expectedRange, statusRange.Description)\n1735\t\t}\n1736\t\n1737\t\t// Add pattern context to expected range\n1738\t\tif rangePattern != \"\" {\n1739\t\t\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s - %s pattern\", expectedRange, rangePattern)\n1740\t\t}\n1741\t\n1742\t\t// Build validation details\n1743\t\tvalidationDetails := []string{\n1744\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Expected range: %d-%d %s\", statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max, statusRange.Description),\n1745\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Actual status code: %d\", actualCode),\n1746\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Distance from valid range: %s\", distanceInfo),\n1747\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern context: %s\", rangePattern),\n1748\t\t}\n1749\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, distanceDetails...)\n1750\t\n1751\t\t// Use FormatValidationErrorWithDetails for consistent formatting\n1752\t\tvalidationErr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n1753\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n1754\t\t\texpectedRange,\n1755\t\t\tactualCode,\n1756\t\t\t\"\", // context - not applicable for range validation\n1757\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet - not needed for status code validation\n1758\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName - not applicable for status code validation\n1759\t\t\t\"\", // location - not applicable for status code validation\n1760\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields - not applicable for status code validation\n1761\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern: %s, Distance: %s\", rangePattern, distanceInfo),\n1762\t\t\texpectedRange,\n1763\t\t\tvalidationDetails,\n1764\t\t)\n1765\t\n1766\t\treturn validationErr.Error()\n1767\t}\n1768\t\n1769\t// getRangePattern identifies the range pattern from a StatusCodeRange.\n1770\t// Returns patterns like '4xx', '5xx', '2xx', etc. based on the range boundaries.\n1771\tfunc getRangePattern(statusRange StatusCodeRange) string {\n1772\t\tmin := statusRange.Min\n1773\t\tmax := statusRange.Max\n1774\t\n1775\t\t// Check for standard century ranges\n1776\t\tif min/100 == max/100 && min%100 == 0 && max%100 == 99 {\n1777\t\t\tcentury := min / 100\n1778\t\t\tswitch century {\n1779\t\t\tcase 1:\n1780\t\t\t\treturn \"1xx (Informational)\"\n1781\t\t\tcase 2:\n1782\t\t\t\treturn \"2xx (Success)\"\n1783\t\t\tcase 3:\n1784\t\t\t\treturn \"3xx (Redirection)\"\n1785\t\t\tcase 4:\n1786\t\t\t\treturn \"4xx (Client Error)\"\n1787\t\t\tcase 5:\n1788\t\t\t\treturn \"5xx (Server Error)\"\n1789\t\t\t}\n1790\t\t}\n1791\t\n1792\t\t// For custom ranges, provide the pattern context\n1793\t\tif min/100 == max/100 {\n1794\t\t\tcentury := min / 100\n1795\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%dxx range (custom)\", century)\n1796\t\t}\n1797\t\n1798\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d range (custom)\", min/100, max/100)\n1799\t}\n1800\t\n1801\t// =============================================================================\n1802\t// ENHANCED ERROR MESSAGE VALIDATION\n1803\t// =============================================================================\n1804\t\n1805\t// EnhancedErrorMessagePattern defines a pattern for matching error messages in response bodies.\n1806\ttype EnhancedErrorMessagePattern struct {\n1807\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check in the response body\n1808\t\tFieldNames *ErrorResponseFieldNames\n1809\t\t// Pattern is a regular expression pattern that the error message must match\n1810\t\tPattern string\n1811\t\t// CaseInsensitive indicates whether pattern matching should be case-insensitive\n1812\t\tCaseInsensitive bool\n1813\t\t// MustContain specifies strings that must be present in the error message\n1814\t\tMustContain []string\n1815\t\t// MustNotContain specifies strings that must NOT be present in the error message\n1816\t\tMustNotContain []string\n1817\t\t// MinLength is the minimum length of the error message (0 for no minimum)\n1818\t\tMinLength int\n1819\t\t// MaxLength is the maximum length of the error message (0 for no maximum)\n1820\t\tMaxLength int\n1821\t}\n1822\t\n1823\t// ErrorMessageValidationResult provides detailed information about error message validation.\n1824\ttype ErrorMessageValidationResult struct {\n1825\t\t// Valid indicates whether the error message validation passed\n1826\t\tValid bool\n1827\t\t// Found indicates whether an error message field was found in the response\n1828\t\tFound bool\n1829\t\t// Message is the actual error message content that was found\n1830\t\tMessage string\n1831\t\t// FieldName is the name of the field where the error message was found\n1832\t\tFieldName string\n1833\t\t// PatternMatched indicates whether the regex pattern matched\n1834\t\tPatternMatched bool\n1835\t\t// MustContainResults shows which required strings were found\n1836\t\tMustContainResults map[string]bool\n1837\t\t// MustNotContainResults shows which forbidden strings were found (should all be false)\n1838\t\tMustNotContainResults map[string]bool\n1839\t\t// LengthValidation indicates whether the message length was valid\n1840\t\tLengthValidation bool\n1841\t\t// Issues contains a list of validation issues found\n1842\t\tIssues []string\n1843\t}\n1844\t\n1845\t// ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails performs comprehensive error message content validation.\n1846\t// It Example usage:\n1855\t//\n1856\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n1857\t//\tpattern := EnhancedErrorMessagePattern{\n1858\t//\t    FieldNames: DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames(),\n1859\t//\t    MustContain: []string{\"invalid\", \"token\"},\n1860\t//\t    MustNotContain: []string{\"password\", \"secret\"},\n1861\t//\t    MinLength: 10,\n1862\t//\t}\n1863\t//\tresult := ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(body, pattern)\n1864\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1865\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Error message validation failed: %v\", result.Issues)\n1866\t//\t}\n1867\tfunc ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(responseBody interface{}, pattern EnhancedErrorMessagePattern) ErrorMessageValidationResult {\n1868\t\tresult := ErrorMessageValidationResult{\n1869\t\t\tValid:                 false,\n1870\t\t\tFound:                 false,\n1871\t\t\tMessage:               \"\",\n1872\t\t\tFieldName:             \"\",\n1873\t\t\tPatternMatched:        false,\n1874\t\t\tMustContainResults:    make(map[string]bool),\n1875\t\t\tMustNotContainResults: make(map[string]bool),\n1876\t\t\tLengthValidation:      true,\n1877\t\t\tIssues:                []string{},\n1878\t\t}\n1879\t\n1880\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n1881\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"response body is nil\")\n1882\t\t\treturn result\n1883\t\t}\n1884\t\n1885\t\t// Use default field names if not provided\n1886\t\tfieldNames := pattern.FieldNames\n1887\t\tif fieldNames == nil {\n1888\t\t\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\n1889\t\t}\n1890\t\n1891\t\t// Try to convert to map\n1892\t\trespMap, ok := responseBody.(map[string]interface{})\n1893\t\tif !ok {\n1894\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"response body is not a map\")\n1895\t\t\treturn result\n1896\t\t}\n1897\t\n1898\t\t// Search for error message in specified fields\n1899\t\tvar foundMessage string\n1900\t\tvar foundFieldName string\n1901\t\n1902\t\t// Check primary field\n1903\t\tif fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName != \"\" {\n1904\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName]; exists {\n1905\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1906\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessage = strValue\n1907\t\t\t\t\tfoundFieldName = fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName\n1908\t\t\t\t}\n1909\t\t\t}\n1910\t\t}\n1911\t\n1912\t\t// Check secondary field if not found in primary\n1913\t\tif foundMessage == \"\" && fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName != \"\" {\n1914\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName]; exists {\n1915\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1916\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessage = strValue\n1917\t\t\t\t\tfoundFieldName = fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName\n1918\t\t\t\t}\n1919\t\t\t}\n1920\t\t}\n1921\t\n1922\t\tresult.Found = foundMessage != \"\"\n1923\t\tresult.Message = foundMessage\n1924\t\tresult.FieldName = foundFieldName\n1925\t\n1926\t\tif !result.Found {\n1927\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"no valid error message field found\")\n1928\t\t\treturn result\n1929\t\t}\n1930\t\n1931\t\t// Validate length\n1932\t\tif pattern.MinLength > 0 && len(foundMessage) < pattern.MinLength {\n1933\t\t\tresult.LengthValidation = false\n1934\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message length %d is less than minimum %d\", len(foundMessage), pattern.MinLength))\n1935\t\t}\n1936\t\n1937\t\tif pattern.MaxLength > 0 && len(foundMessage) > pattern.MaxLength {\n1938\t\t\tresult.LengthValidation = false\n1939\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message length %d exceeds maximum %d\", len(foundMessage), pattern.MaxLength))\n1940\t\t}\n1941\t\n1942\t\t// Validate regex pattern\n1943\t\tif pattern.Pattern != \"\" {\n1944\t\t\tregex := regexp.MustCompile(pattern.Pattern)\n1945\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1946\t\t\t\tregex = regexp.MustCompile(\"(?i)\" + pattern.Pattern)\n1947\t\t\t}\n1948\t\t\tresult.PatternMatched = regex.MatchString(foundMessage)\n1949\t\t\tif !result.PatternMatched {\n1950\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message does not match required pattern: %s\", pattern.Pattern))\n1951\t\t\t}\n1952\t\t} else {\n1953\t\t\tresult.PatternMatched = true // No pattern specified, consider it matched\n1954\t\t}\n1955\t\n1956\t\t// Validate MustContain strings\n1957\t\tallMustContainFound := true\n1958\t\tfor _, mustContain := range pattern.MustContain {\n1959\t\t\tvar found bool\n1960\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1961\t\t\t\t// Case-insensitive search\n1962\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustContain))\n1963\t\t\t} else {\n1964\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustContain)\n1965\t\t\t}\n1966\t\t\tresult.MustContainResults[mustContain] = found\n1967\t\t\tif !found {\n1968\t\t\t\tallMustContainFound = false\n1969\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message does not contain required string: %s\", mustContain))\n1970\t\t\t}\n1971\t\t}\n1972\t\n1973\t\t// Validate MustNotContain strings\n1974\t\thasForbiddenStrings := false\n1975\t\tfor _, mustNotContain := range pattern.MustNotContain {\n1976\t\t\tvar found bool\n1977\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1978\t\t\t\t// Case-insensitive search\n1979\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustNotContain))\n1980\t\t\t} else {\n1981\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustNotContain)\n1982\t\t\t}\n1983\t\t\tresult.MustNotContainResults[mustNotContain] = found\n1984\t\t\tif found {\n1985\t\t\t\thasForbiddenStrings = true\n1986\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message contains forbidden string: %s\", mustNotContain))\n1987\t\t\t}\n1988\t\t}\n1989\t\n1990\t\t// Overall validity\n1991\t\tresult.Valid = result.Found && result.LengthValidation && result.PatternMatched &&\n1992\t\t\tallMustContainFound && !hasForbiddenStrings\n1993\t\n1994\t\treturn result\n1995\t}\n1996\t\n1997\t// =============================================================================\n1998\t// ERROR CODE DETECTION\n1999\t// =============================================================================\n2000\t\n2001\t// ErrorCodeMatch represents a found error code in a response.\n2002\ttype ErrorCodeMatch struct {\n2003\t\t// FieldName is the name of the field where the error code was found\n2004\t\tFieldName string\n2005\t\t// CodeValue is the actual error code value (as a string for flexibility)\n2006\t\tCodeValue string\n2007\t\t// NumericCode is the error code parsed as an integer (if applicable)\n2008\t\tNumericCode *int\n2009\t\t// MatchedPattern is the pattern that matched this code\n2010\t\tMatchedPattern string\n2011\t\t// Position describes where in the response the code was found\n2012\t\tPosition string\n2013\t}\n2014\t\n2015\t// EnhancedErrorCodePattern defines a pattern for finding error codes in responses.\n2016\ttype EnhancedErrorCodePattern struct {\n2017\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check for error codes\n2018\t\tFieldNames []string\n2019\t\t// NumericOnly indicates whether to only match numeric error codes\n2020\t\tNumericOnly bool\n2021\t\t// ValuePatterns contains regex patterns for valid error code values\n2022\t\tValuePatterns []string\n2023\t}\n2024\t\n2025\t// FindErrorCodesInResponse searches for error codes in an HTTP response body.\n2026\t// This is useful for validating that specific error codes are present in error responses.\n2027\t//\n2028\t// Parameters:\n2029\t//   - responseBody: The parsed response body (should be a map[string]interface{})\n2030\t//   - patterns: The patterns to use for finding error codes\n2031\t//\n2032\t// Returns a slice of ErrorCodeMatch representing all found error codes.\n2033\t//\n2034\t// Example usage:\n2035\t//\n2036\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n2037\t//\tpatterns := []EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n2038\t//\t    {\n2039\t//\t        FieldNames: []string{\"error_code\", \"code\"},\n2040\t//\t        NumericOnly: true,\n2041\t//\t    },\n2042\t//\t    {\n2043\t//\t        FieldNames: []string{\"error\"},\n2044\t//\t        ValuePatterns: []string{\"invalid_token\", \"access_denied\"},\n2045\t//\t    },\n2046\t//\t}\n2047\t//\tmatches := FindErrorCodesInResponse(body, patterns)\n2048\t//\tfor _, match := range matches {\n2049\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Found error code: %s in field: %s\", match.CodeValue, match.FieldName)\n2050\t//\t}\n2051\tfunc FindErrorCodesInResponse(responseBody interface{}, patterns []EnhancedErrorCodePattern) []ErrorCodeMatch {\n2052\t\tvar matches []ErrorCodeMatch\n2053\t\n2054\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n2055\t\t\treturn matches\n2056\t\t}\n2057\t\n2058\t\t// Try to convert to map\n2059\t\trespMap, ok := responseBody.(map[string]interface{})\n2060\t\tif !ok {\n2061\t\t\treturn matches\n2062\t\t}\n2063\t\n2064\t\tfor _, pattern := range patterns {\n2065\t\t\t// Default to common error code fields if none specified\n2066\t\t\tfieldsToCheck := pattern.FieldNames\n2067\t\t\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\n2068\t\t\t\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\"error_code\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"}\n2069\t\t\t}\n2070\t\n2071\t\t\tfor _, fieldName := range fieldsToCheck {\n2072\t\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldName]; exists {\n2073\t\t\t\t\tvar codeValue string\n2074\t\t\t\t\tvar numericCode *int\n2075\t\t\t\t\tvar matchedPattern string\n2076\t\n2077\t\t\t\t\t// Handle different value types\n2078\t\t\t\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n2079\t\t\t\t\tcase string:\n2080\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = v\n2081\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"string\"\n2082\t\t\t\t\t\t// Try to parse as number\n2083\t\t\t\t\t\tif num, err := parseIntFromString(v); err == nil && pattern.NumericOnly {\n2084\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &num\n2085\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n2086\t\t\t\t\tcase int:\n2087\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", v)\n2088\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &v\n2089\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"numeric\"\n2090\t\t\t\t\tcase float64:\n2091\t\t\t\t\t\t// JSON numbers are often parsed as float64\n2092\t\t\t\t\t\tif v == float64(int(v)) {\n2093\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnum := int(v)\n2094\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", num)\n2095\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &num\n2096\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"numeric\"\n2097\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n2098\t\t\t\t\tcase bool:\n2099\t\t\t\t\t\t// Some APIs use true/false as error codes\n2100\t\t\t\t\t\tif v {\n2101\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = \"true\"\n2102\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"boolean\"\n2103\t\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n2104\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = \"false\"\n2105\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"boolean\"\n2106\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n2107\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n2108\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue // Skip unsupported types\n2109\t\t\t\t\t}\n2110\t\n2111\t\t\t\t\t// Validate against value patterns if specified\n2112\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched := true\n2113\t\t\t\t\tif len(pattern.ValuePatterns) > 0 {\n2114\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = false\n2115\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, valuePattern := range pattern.ValuePatterns {\n2116\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatched, _ := regexp.MatchString(valuePattern, codeValue)\n2117\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif matched {\n2118\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = true\n2119\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = valuePattern\n2120\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n2121\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n2122\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n2123\t\t\t\t\t}\n2124\t\n2125\t\t\t\t\t// Check numeric-only constraint\n2126\t\t\t\t\tif pattern.NumericOnly && numericCode == nil {\n2127\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = false\n2128\t\t\t\t\t}\n2129\t\n2130\t\t\t\t\tif patternsMatched && codeValue != \"\" {\n2131\t\t\t\t\t\tmatches = append(matches, ErrorCodeMatch{\n2132\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFieldName:      fieldName,\n2133\t\t\t\t\t\t\tCodeValue:      codeValue,\n2134\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNumericCode:    numericCode,\n2135\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMatchedPattern: matchedPattern,\n2136\t\t\t\t\t\t\tPosition:       fmt.Sprintf(\"field '%s' in response body\", fieldName),\n2137\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n2138\t\t\t\t\t}\n2139\t\t\t\t}\n2140\t\t\t}\n2141\t\t}\n2142\t\n2143\t\treturn matches\n2144\t}\n2145\t\n2146\t// ValidateErrorCodeInResponse checks if a specific error code is present in a response.\n2147\t// This is a convenience wrapper around FindErrorCodesInResponse for checking a single code.\n2148\t//\n2149\t// Parameters:\n2150\t//   - responseBody: The parseJSON(response.Body)\n2159\t//\thasInvalidToken := ValidateErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"invalid_token\", \"error\")\n2160\t//\tif hasInvalidToken {\n2161\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Response contains invalid_token error\")\n2162\t//\t}\n2163\tfunc ValidateErrorCodeInResponse(responseBody interface{}, expectedCode string, fieldName string) bool {\n2164\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n2165\t\t\treturn false\n2166\t\t}\n2167\t\n2168\t\tvar pattern EnhancedErrorCodePattern\n2169\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n2170\t\t\tpattern = EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n2171\t\t\t\tFieldNames:
